API Explorer

Interactive API documentation powered by Swagger UI. Test endpoints directly from this interface.

Architecture Diagrams

Interactive system architecture diagrams rendered with Mermaid.

Interactive Code Examples

Copy-to-clipboard code examples for common use cases.

API Authentication

Example of how to authenticate with the API using JWT tokens.

import requests
import json

# Authentication endpoint
auth_url = "http://localhost:8000/api/v1/auth/login"

# Login credentials
credentials = {
    "username": "your_username",
    "password": "your_password"
}

# Get access token
response = requests.post(auth_url, json=credentials)
token = response.json()["access_token"]

# Use token in subsequent requests
headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
api_response = requests.get(
    "http://localhost:8000/api/v1/retail/sales", 
    headers=headers
)

GraphQL Query

Example GraphQL query for retrieving sales data with filters.

query GetSalesData($startDate: String!, $endDate: String!) {
  sales(
    filters: {
      date_range: {
        start: $startDate
        end: $endDate
      }
      status: "completed"
    }
  ) {
    id
    customer {
      name
      email
      segment
    }
    products {
      name
      category
      price
      quantity
    }
    total_amount
    order_date
    shipping_address {
      city
      country
    }
  }
}

ETL Pipeline Trigger

Example of how to trigger an ETL pipeline programmatically.

import requests
from datetime import datetime

# ETL pipeline endpoint
pipeline_url = "http://localhost:8000/api/v1/etl/pipelines/retail-etl/trigger"

# Pipeline parameters
params = {
    "environment": "production",
    "source_date": datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d"),
    "full_refresh": False,
    "notify_on_completion": True
}

# Trigger pipeline
response = requests.post(
    pipeline_url, 
    json=params,
    headers=headers
)

# Monitor pipeline status
pipeline_id = response.json()["pipeline_id"]
status_url = f"http://localhost:8000/api/v1/etl/pipelines/{pipeline_id}/status"

status_response = requests.get(status_url, headers=headers)
print(f"Pipeline Status: {status_response.json()['status']}")

Real-time Monitoring

Example of connecting to real-time monitoring websocket.

// WebSocket connection for real-time monitoring
const ws = new WebSocket('ws://localhost:8000/api/v1/monitoring/realtime');

ws.onopen = function(event) {
    console.log('Connected to monitoring stream');
    
    // Subscribe to specific metrics
    ws.send(JSON.stringify({
        action: 'subscribe',
        metrics: ['pipeline_status', 'system_health', 'error_rate']
    }));
};

ws.onmessage = function(event) {
    const data = JSON.parse(event.data);
    
    switch(data.type) {
        case 'pipeline_status':
            updatePipelineStatus(data.payload);
            break;
        case 'system_health':
            updateSystemHealth(data.payload);
            break;
        case 'error_rate':
            updateErrorRate(data.payload);
            break;
    }
};

function updatePipelineStatus(status) {
    document.getElementById('pipeline-status').innerText = status.status;
    document.getElementById('pipeline-progress').value = status.progress;
}
